finding_results.notes: Replaced all three converging abstract diagnoses with concrete observed behavior differentiated by voice. Nate (line 241) now over-describes a specific dinner moment: Cliff leaning into Tommy's ear, Tommy not moving, not pushing back, holding his fork and staring at his plate, with Nate's characteristic self-correction ('And Tommy always--I mean, you met him Saturday'). Joel (lines 309, 325, 331) now provides a factual behavioral shift observation: Cliff stopped bringing up the forty thousand publicly and switched to private conversations with Tommy, leading Joel to infer Cliff found something in the study. Also fixed an internal repetition where Joel's study-question answer (line 309, previously 'Cliff gets louder when he wants money. Today he was smooth. Tommy was quiet') duplicated his later tension description (line 321); the study-question answer is now terse and distinct ('Because forty thousand makes Cliff loud. This was quiet'). Paul (lines 389, 393) now gives a timed, placement-anchored observation: Tommy checking the slider lock twice after the eight o'clock deck conversation, then standing in the kitchen with his hands on the counter doing nothing for a minute, with Paul's risk-awareness surfacing naturally ('You can't miss a man checking a lock twice in a house where somebody already died'). Removed the narrator line that admired Paul's dialogue ('That sounded closer to Joel than to Paul, which made her trust it more') since Paul's revised text now sounds naturally like Paul rather than like Joel. Two of three witnesses now provide concrete observed beats (Nate: movement and non-reaction at dinner; Paul: timed location-shift and lock-checking); Joel's observation is grounded in specific behavioral change rather than an abstract label.

summary: Addressed the single finding by breaking apart the three-witness convergence on abstract Tommy-Cliff diagnosis. Nate now gives rambling concrete observation of a dinner moment with characteristic self-correction. Joel now cites a specific behavioral shift (Cliff's public-to-private strategy change) and ties it to the study, replacing the polished 'Cliff had leverage. Tommy knew it' label. Paul now anchors his concern in a timed, placed, concrete observation (Tommy checking the slider lock twice at eight o'clock) rather than delivering a quotable insight. Each witness's revised lines stay within their style-bible voice profile: Nate over-describes and lists, Joel notes factual inconsistency and gives a directive, Paul anchors in time and placement with insurance-adjacent risk awareness. Collateral fix: removed Joel's duplicated smooth/quiet observation that appeared in both his study-question answer and his tension description.
